meaning: n.泥沼,蜕的皮,腐肉vt.使陷入泥沼,使沉沦,脱落,抛弃vi.在泥浆中跋涉,蜕皮,脱落
examples: Our bodies slough off dead cells.
He was sinking into the Slough of Despond.
All reptiles have to slough their skin to grow.
To get out on the water, organise a wildlife cruise with Elkhorn Slough Safari.
But for guaranteed otter spotting in the wild, head to Elkhorn Slough National Estuarine Research Reserve.
